title: Nephrodium borneense
taxonomy: Nephrodium borneense is classified as a species of extinct fern within the family Matoniaaceae. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta and is known to be a member of the order Matoniales. The species Nephrodium borneense is considered to be a fossil fern, with its existence dating back to the Eocene epoch. It is part of the family Matoniaaceae, which is a family of ferns that are primarily found in tropical regions. The classification of Nephrodium borneense is significant in understanding the evolutionary history of ferns during the Eocene epoch.
uses: Nephrodium borneense, a plant species found in Borneo, has been reported to have various medicinal properties. intro: Nephrodium borneense, also known as Borneo maidenhair, is a species of fern native to the tropical forests of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium borneense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
